content:
There's a growing disconnect in how people perceive AI capabilities, largely shaped by which models they've used and in what context. Many base their views on outdated or free versions like ChatGPT’s older tiers, while others using cutting-edge, paid models in technical fields see transformative progress.

• Free and outdated models like basic ChatGPT or Advanced Voice Mode create misleading impressions due to glitches and limitations not present in current state-of-the-art systems.  
• Latest agentic models such as OpenAI Codex and Claude Code show dramatic advances, especially in programming, math, and research, where results can be verified and rewarded precisely.  
• High-end AI capabilities are "peaky"—concentrated in technical domains because reinforcement learning works best there and because these areas offer greater business value.  
• This leads to two disconnected perspectives: one group underestimates AI due to limited exposure, while professionals using advanced models witness near-magical performance, fueling vastly different beliefs about AI's real potential.
